rulings.Case Title: PHYLLIS RUSSELL et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, SEPTEMBER 24, 2007, LINE 16. DEFENDANT FOSTER WHEELER, L.L.C.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED, TEN DAYS TO ANSWER. THE DEMURRER IS TIMELY BUT THE ARGUMENTS ARE WITHOUT MERIT. ROGER'S GALVANIZING IS NOT A FEDERAL ENCLAVE. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: PHYLLIS RUSSELL et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS H. LUTGE VS. MICHAEL C. MILLER et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, SEPTEMBER 24, 2007, LINE 1. DEFENDANT MICHAEL MILLER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED, FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. THE DECISIONS IN GONZALES V SUPERIOR COURT (1987) 189 CAL. APP. 3D 1542 AND HOMESTEAD V SUPERIOR COURT (1986) 179 CAL. APP. 3D 494 MAKE CLEAR THAT A MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGEMENT MUST BE DENIED WHERE THE MOTION FAILS TO DISPOSE OF EACH OF THE CAUSES OF ACTION. SUCH A MOTION CANNOT BE CONVERTED TO A MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. GIVEN THE COURT'S RULING, THERE IS NO NEED TO ADDRESS THE PARTIES' EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: CHERYL MORONG VS. ALLIED PACKING et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, SEPTEMBER 24, 2007, LINE 2. DEFENDANT OCCIDENTAL PETROLEUM CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: NATALIE PERTSOVSKY VS. PETER CHASE et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, SEPTEMBER 24, 2007, LINE 3. DEFENDANT LILIANA BUCAY, D.D.S.' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF BASED ON THE JACOBSON DECLARATION. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT IN THAT PLAINTIFF DID NOT PROVIDE A CONTRARY EXPERT OPINION, AND THE ADEQUACY PLAINTIFF'S TREATMENT REQUIRED ON EXPERT OPINION. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM ALSO IS BARRED BY STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS, C.C.P. SECTION 340.5, AND PLAINTIFF WAS ON NOTICE OF HER CLAIM AS EARLY AS 1996 AND CERTAINLY IN OCTOBER OF 2001 WHEN SHE SOUGHT TREATMENT FROM DR. KAMANIAN. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: NOURREDDINE CHAKROUN VS. SLIMANE, LLC, DBA CREPES A GO-G0., AN ENTITY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, SEPTEMBER 24, 2007, LINE 4. DEFENDANTS THE CREPES A GO GO GROUP, LIMITED LIABILITY, CAFE MANAGEMENT ASSOCIATES, INC., TELEGRAPH AVENUE CAFE, INC., CAFE ADMINISTRATION CORPORATION AND CREPESMAKERS, INC. AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND WITHIN TEN DAYS TO PLEAD FACTS, RATHER THAN CONCLUSIONS WITH RESPECT TO FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION. MOTION TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. =(302/PJM/SN-X)

rulings.Case Title: PENELOPE ROBINSON VS. ROBYN KAUFMAN et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, SEPTEMBER 24, 2007, LINE 5. DEFENDANT CRESCENT HEIGHTS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED AS TO THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION. COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT KAUFMAN WAS THE OWNER OF THE UNIT AND MADE THE REPRESENTATIONS. ALTHOUGH THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT KAUFMAN WAS AN AGENT OF THE OTHER DEFENDANTS, THE ALLEGATIONS DO NOT SUPPORT THE ASSERTION THAT KAUFMAN WAS ACTING AS AN AGENT IN CONNECTION WITH THE SALE OF THIS UNIT. TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. OVERRULED AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION BASED ON PARAGRAPH SIXTY-EIGHT. SUSTAINED AS TO THE NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION, FAILURE TO PLEAD FACTS ESTABLISHING A CONTRACTUAL RELATIONSHIP WITH THESE DEFENDANTS. THE ONLY CONTRACTUAL RELATIONSHIP ALLEGED IS WITH KAUFMAN, TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. OVERRULED AS TO THE EIGHTH, TENTH AND ELEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION. S.B. 800 DOES NOT LIMIT CAUSES OF ACTION, RATHER IT DEFINES THE APPLICABLE STANDARDS. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL A ZANONI VS. JOHN R MONTGOMERY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Reconsider Order Denying Antislapp Motion And Demurrer To First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, SEPTEMBER 24, 2007, LINE 6. DEFENDANT JOHN MONTGOMERY'S Motion To Reconsider Order Denying Anti-SLAPP Motion And Demurrer To First Amended Complaint IS OFF CALENDAR, MATTER TO BE RE-NOTICED BEFORE JUDGE ALVARADO WHO HEARD THE UNDERLYING MOTION. HE IS HEARING CASES AT THE POLK STREET ANNEX. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD L MILLER VS. BERNARD N WOLF et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, SEPTEMBER 24, 2007, LINE 17. DEFENDANT BERNARD N. WOLF'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. SINCE DEFENDANT DID NOT REPRESENT PLAINTIFF DURING THE REMAND PROCEEDINGS, THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS RAN FROM THE DATE OF THE FIRST COURT OF APPEAL DECISION. ALTERNATIVELY, DEFENDANT'S REPRESENTATION WAS TERMINATED BY PLAINTIFF ON MARCH 20, 2006 AND THIS SUIT WAS NOT FILED UNTIL MARCH 27, 2007. THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DISGORGEMENT IS BASED UPON PLAINTIFF'S LEGAL MALPRACTICE CLAIM AND IS BARRED BY THE ONE YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS, C.C.P. SECTION 340.6. FURTHER, IT IS UNLIKELY THAT PLAINTIFF CAN STATE A CLAIM BASED UPON DEFENDANT'S PREPARATION OF THE STATEMENT OF DECISION AND THE ALLEGATIONS RELATED TO HANDLING THE APPEAL LACK THE REQUISITE SPECIFICITY. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Seeking Determination That The Automatic Stay Of 11 U.S.C. 365(A) Applies To Co-Deft
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, SEPTEMBER 24, 2007, LINE 9. DEFENDANT PLEASANT CARE PROTECTION'S Motion Seeking Determination That The Automatic Stay Of 11 U.S.C. 365(A) Applies To Co-DEFENDANT IS DENIED BASED ON COMITY. THE BANKRUPTCY COURT IS IN A FAR BETTER POSITION THAN THIS COURT TO DETERMINE THE SCOPE OF THE STAY AND THE NEED FOR PROTECTION OF THE BANKRUPTCY COURT. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, SEPTEMBER 24, 2007, LINE 15. DEFENDANTS DEBORAH DOYLE AND MELVIN WHARTNABY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED, FIVE DAYS TO ANSWER.
